meta.title: Zana Codebase Hub
meta.subtitle: A repo-local map of the live Tauri app, voice pipeline, docs drift, release gates, and manual QA surfaces.

repository.workspaceNote: Root Cargo.toml lists only src-tauri as a workspace member. Treat root src/ as a stale trap unless reactivated intentionally.

summary.label: authoritative runtime
summary.detail: Tauri 2 app, Rust backend, vanilla src-ui assets

summary.label: primary risk
summary.detail: privacy, version, egui migration, release docs

pages.title: orientation
pages.summary: Use this first. It separates the live Tauri path from stale root and planning surfaces.

pages.title: voice pipeline
pages.summary: The critical product flow: trigger key, capture audio, local Whisper, optional rewrite, final paste.

pages.title: preferences and rewrite
pages.summary: Settings bridge between src-ui/preferences.html and typed backend validation.

pages.title: ui surfaces
pages.summary: Vanilla HTML/JS surfaces loaded by Tauri windows and the macOS NSPanel orb.

pages.title: hooks and plugins
pages.summary: Event bus, system handlers, manifest parsing, plugin registry, and the current placeholder loader boundary.

pages.title: release and packaging
pages.summary: CI, release workflow, manual macOS scripts, bundle identity, and launch smoke risks.

pages.title: docs drift
pages.summary: Public docs, stale egui migration docs, privacy mismatch, install guide cleanup, and QA gaps.

flows.title: hotkey record to paste
flows.summary: macOS Fn and Windows Ctrl paths start capture, stop capture, transcribe, paste response.text, and hide the orb.

steps.label: trigger
steps.summary: main.rs monitors Fn/Ctrl press, double-tap latch, and release.

steps.label: start
steps.summary: start_recording_inner captures clipboard before audio capture begins.

steps.label: stop
steps.summary: stop_recording_inner captures screenshot if enabled and stores audio.

steps.label: paste
steps.summary: transcribe returns final text; hotkey path pastes response.text.

flows.title: local Whisper then optional rewrite
flows.summary: Local STT is mandatory. Cloud rewrite is opt-in, HTTPS or localhost, and falls back to raw transcript.

steps.label: model check
steps.summary: Whisper model must exist in local cache before transcription.

steps.label: local stt
steps.summary: WhisperEngine transcribes local audio samples first.

steps.label: rewrite
steps.summary: Optional provider receives transcript, clipboard, profile, memory, and optional screenshot.

steps.label: fallback
steps.summary: Rewrite errors keep success true and paste corrected local text.

flows.title: first-run onboarding
flows.summary: First run shows onboarding, asks for accessibility, downloads Small Whisper model, marks complete, then exits.

steps.label: detect
steps.summary: onboarding marker determines whether normal startup continues.

steps.label: permission
steps.summary: macOS accessibility pane can be opened from onboarding.

steps.label: model
steps.summary: Small Whisper model downloads with progress events.

steps.label: exit
steps.summary: Completion marker is written and the app exits for restart.

flows.title: plugin discovery and hooks
flows.summary: Plugin directories with plugin.toml load into registry and attach hook adapters. Actual dynamic code loading is still a placeholder boundary.

steps.label: discover
steps.summary: PluginManager scans the configured plugins directory.

steps.label: validate
steps.summary: PluginManifest is parsed and validated.

steps.label: register
steps.summary: Registry stores metadata and enabled state.

steps.label: hook
steps.summary: PluginHookAdapter registers with the EventBus.

flows.title: release candidate gates
flows.summary: CI gates compile/test/clippy. Release builds macOS and Windows artifacts, but local app launch and macOS permissions still need manual smoke.

steps.label: verify
steps.summary: fmt, cargo check, cargo test, clippy run on macOS.

steps.label: bundle
steps.summary: Release workflow builds universal Zana.app and DMG.

steps.label: sign
steps.summary: Developer ID signing and notarization run only when secrets exist.

steps.label: smoke
steps.summary: Visible runtime is tray/orb-driven, so green build is not enough.

objects.title: workspace state
objects.recommendation: keep as the first mental model for every future task
objects.summary: Root Cargo.toml declares only src-tauri as the active workspace member.
objects.evidence: The sibling src/ crate and root tests are present but not exercised by the root workspace.
flags: do not treat root src/ as live without explicit reactivation

objects.title: active Tauri crate
objects.recommendation: keep
objects.summary: Zana-app is the package built, tested, and released by CI.
objects.evidence: CI and release workflows run cargo commands against -p Zana-app.
flags: Tauri updater dependency exists but updater plugin is disabled

objects.title: root src/ stale crate
objects.recommendation: archive or clearly label before anyone edits it
objects.summary: The root src/ tree looks like an egui or older app path and is not listed in the active workspace.
objects.evidence: Root Cargo.toml has members = ["src-tauri"].
flags: root tests likely target old gui modules
keep visible as legacy until intentionally deleted

objects.title: main Tauri entry point
objects.recommendation: keep, but treat hotkey changes as manual-QA required
objects.summary: Initializes app state, windows, tray menu, command handlers, Fn/Ctrl monitors, orb visibility, and paste flow.
objects.evidence: Registers commands and owns macOS NSPanel plus Windows orb behavior.
flags: visible runtime is tray/orb driven
compile success does not prove user can see the app

objects.title: settings and recording context
objects.recommendation: keep and protect privacy defaults
objects.summary: AppState owns settings, audio capture, Whisper engine, plugin manager, captured audio, clipboard snapshot, and screenshot snapshot.
objects.evidence: Cloud rewrite defaults off; clipboard and screenshot are per-recording context and cleared after use.
flags: cloud rewrite must stay opt-in
screenshot context is macOS only and setting-gated

objects.title: audio commands
objects.recommendation: keep
objects.summary: Tauri command layer for device listing, start, stop, metrics, and recording state.
objects.evidence: start_recording_inner captures clipboard before capture; stop_recording_inner stores audio and captures screenshot if enabled.
flags: microphone permission and hardware behavior are runtime-only

objects.title: cpal audio capture
objects.recommendation: keep
objects.summary: Captures microphone audio, exposes levels/FFT, and resamples to 16 kHz for Whisper.
objects.evidence: Hardware and permission behavior cannot be proven by static contract tests.
flags: needs mic permission smoke
needs real device smoke

objects.title: transcription and rewrite pipeline
objects.recommendation: keep as product-critical
objects.summary: Lists/downloads models, transcribes captured audio locally, applies dictionary replacements, optionally rewrites, and returns final paste text.
objects.evidence: Local Whisper runs before run_cloud_rewrite. Rewrite failure sets rewrite_error and leaves final text as local text.
flags: do not make cloud rewrite mandatory
paste path must use response.text

objects.title: preferences command DTO
objects.recommendation: keep
objects.summary: Maps preferences UI camelCase fields to Settings, validates rewrite provider settings, and persists the config.
objects.evidence: Rewrite URL validation allows HTTPS or localhost/127.0.0.1 only.
flags: remote rewrite requires API key
writing profile fields cannot be empty

objects.title: Whisper engine
objects.recommendation: keep
objects.summary: Manages model enum, model download URLs, local model cache, context cache, and transcription.
objects.evidence: Models live under local Zana data directory and are downloaded from Hugging Face.
flags: first transcription depends on model cache
network is only required for model download unless rewrite is enabled

objects.title: hook event bus
objects.recommendation: keep
objects.summary: Typed events, handler registration, priority dispatch, and system handlers for logging, metrics, and validation.
objects.evidence: Plugins attach through PluginHookAdapter and depend on event compatibility.
flags: event contract drift affects plugins

objects.title: plugin manager
objects.recommendation: document placeholder boundary before selling it as complete
objects.summary: Discovers plugin directories, parses manifests, registers plugin metadata, and attaches hook adapters.
objects.evidence: create_plugin_instance is a placeholder; this is not a fully dynamic plugin runtime yet.
flags: plugin docs overstate marketplace/publish surfaces
loader is metadata and hook adapter, not arbitrary plugin code execution

objects.title: plugin IPC commands
objects.recommendation: review before exposing install/uninstall broadly
objects.summary: Lists, enables, disables, installs, and uninstalls plugins from filesystem paths.
objects.evidence: uninstall_plugin removes plugin directories; keep UI actions as signals unless explicitly approved.
flags: contains recursive directory removal in product code
needs careful UX and confirmation if user-facing

objects.title: first-run onboarding backend
objects.recommendation: keep
objects.summary: Checks first-run marker, accessibility permission, opens macOS Settings, downloads Small model, and marks onboarding complete.
objects.evidence: Normal Fn setup is skipped during onboarding.
flags: complete_onboarding_and_exit exits app for restart

objects.title: diagnostics command
objects.recommendation: keep and consider surfacing in support flow
objects.summary: Checks runtime health areas such as accessibility, audio, model status, and settings paths.
objects.evidence: Most diagnostics are runtime/environment-dependent and not captured by static tests.
flags: useful for first-run and release smoke triage

objects.title: Tauri bundle config
objects.recommendation: preserve identity unless explicitly approved
objects.summary: Defines productName Zana, identifier app.zana, macOS signing identity, bundle targets, tray icon, and security capabilities.
objects.evidence: app.zana identity is important for macOS permissions continuity.
flags: tauri config version is 1.0.0 while workspace package is 0.1.0

objects.title: Tauri capabilities
objects.recommendation: review permission scope before public release
objects.summary: Capabilities cover main, preferences, onboarding, and orb windows with FS, shell, dialog, event, and drag permissions.
objects.evidence: Contract tests guard preferences and orb drag capability.
flags: default capability includes broad fs allow-read and allow-write

objects.title: preferences UI
objects.recommendation: keep and test with backend command changes
objects.summary: Vanilla preferences window exposes model, input, trigger, orb style, rewrite provider, screenshot, and writing profile fields.
objects.evidence: Saves through save_preferences and maps camelCase fields to Rust Preferences.
flags: API key is stored locally in Zana settings
provider fields must stay aligned with commands/settings.rs

objects.title: orb UI and lifecycle
objects.recommendation: keep
objects.summary: Orb window and lifecycle scripts render the visual recording surface and drag target.
objects.evidence: main.rs creates orb window or macOS NSPanel and relays events into it.
flags: fullscreen overlay and compact hitbox require visual smoke
drag target covered by static contract test

objects.title: onboarding UI
objects.recommendation: keep but verify against first-run state
objects.summary: First-run HTML guides accessibility permission and Whisper model download.
objects.evidence: Onboarding window is created only when onboarding::is_first_run returns true.
flags: first-run marker state affects whether this appears

objects.title: legacy main UI surface
objects.recommendation: review, label, or remove from public docs if not launched
objects.summary: src-ui/index.html and app.js look like a legacy/dev surface. Startup creates onboarding, preferences, about, and orb windows, not a persistent main window.
objects.evidence: tauri.conf.json has windows = [] and main.rs builds windows programmatically.
flags: do not cite as live primary UI without proving a window path

objects.title: active contract tests
objects.recommendation: keep and extend for docs drift critical paths
objects.summary: Static integration-style tests verify Tauri capabilities, preferences fields, bundle identity, update banner, and orb geometry.
objects.evidence: Lives under src-tauri/tests and is exercised by cargo test -p Zana-app.
flags: does not prove microphone, accessibility, paste, model download, or GUI launch behavior

objects.title: root integration tests
objects.recommendation: archive or port if still valuable
objects.summary: Root tests/integration.rs appears tied to the stale root src/ crate and not the active workspace member.
objects.evidence: Root workspace only includes src-tauri.
flags: easy to mistake for active coverage

objects.title: README
objects.recommendation: keep current before public release
objects.summary: README now orients public readers to the active Zana build, release, and privacy posture.
objects.evidence: Broken demo reference and coming-soon model language were removed; current public docs are linked from docs/README.md.
flags: keep version metadata aligned before tagging
keep optional rewrite privacy copy in sync with runtime

objects.title: privacy policy
objects.recommendation: keep aligned with opt-in rewrite behavior
objects.summary: PRIVACY.md now distinguishes local transcription from opt-in rewrite provider calls.
objects.evidence: The policy discloses model downloads, GitHub update checks, and optional rewrite provider requests.
flags: must distinguish local transcription from opt-in cloud rewrite
must disclose optional screenshot context

objects.title: INSTALL guide
objects.recommendation: fix before handing to normal users
objects.summary: INSTALL.md is mostly a source-build guide and includes destructive uninstall shell examples.
objects.evidence: Release install path is not yet the main guide despite release workflow and scripts existing.
flags: destructive examples should be softened or clearly warned
normal install vs developer build should be separated

objects.title: QA checklist
objects.recommendation: extend for current release risks
objects.summary: QA_CHECKLIST.md covers core surfaces but misses rewrite, screenshot, update check, and packaging smoke specifics.
objects.evidence: Live preferences now expose rewrite provider and screenshot fields.
flags: add opt-in rewrite and local-only negative tests
add update banner and GitHub release check

objects.title: egui migration docs
objects.recommendation: archive or label as historical, not current architecture
objects.summary: ARCHITECTURE, MIGRATION, and API docs contain egui/pure Rust claims that do not match the live Tauri and src-ui runtime.
objects.evidence: tauri.conf.json frontendDist points to ../src-ui and the active crate depends on Tauri.
flags: can mislead future agents into editing wrong code
do not use as proof that code exists

objects.title: completed planning docs
objects.recommendation: move to archive or annotate with current status
objects.summary: Integration and completion specs describe work that is partly implemented now, so their task language reads stale.
objects.evidence: Live AppState already has PluginManager and plugin commands, despite docs saying they need wiring.
flags: keep as history, not authoritative runtime docs

objects.title: onboarding plan
objects.recommendation: keep with status annotation
objects.summary: Onboarding plan broadly matches live onboarding window and commands but should say what is shipped versus planned.
objects.evidence: src-ui/onboarding.html and onboarding commands exist.
flags: avoid treating full plan as exact shipped behavior

objects.title: plugin docs
objects.recommendation: trim claims to what the runtime actually supports
objects.summary: Plugin docs are useful but mix real manifest/hook behavior with marketplace or publish claims that are not proven by the runtime.
objects.evidence: PluginManager loads manifests and placeholder instances; marketplace publish path is not live proof.
flags: separate supported local plugin manifests from aspirational marketplace

objects.title: GitHub CI and release workflows
objects.recommendation: keep, but add artifact smoke evidence before declaring release ready
objects.summary: CI runs fmt, check, tests, clippy, and Windows check. Release builds macOS universal DMG and Windows NSIS, then creates a GitHub release.
objects.evidence: Release workflow signs and notarizes only when secrets are present.
flags: CI green does not prove visible tray/orb runtime
artifact launch smoke still needed

objects.title: macOS build and notarization scripts
objects.recommendation: keep and document environment caveats
objects.summary: Manual scripts build universal macOS bundles, merge binaries, sign, create DMG, and notarize.
objects.evidence: Prior memory shows hdiutil create can fail in this environment and app bundle smoke can be blocked by LaunchServices.
flags: hdiutil may fail depending on environment
capture exact artifact paths when validating

objects.title: manual update check
objects.recommendation: document as update check, not auto-update
objects.summary: Preferences can check latest GitHub release. The Tauri updater plugin is present but commented out.
objects.evidence: update.rs fetches GitHub releases/latest while main.rs comments out tauri updater plugin.
flags: do not call this auto-update until updater is enabled

objects.title: version drift
objects.recommendation: resolve before tagging
objects.summary: Cargo workspace version, Tauri config version, and README project status disagree.
objects.evidence: Cargo.toml says 0.1.0, tauri.conf.json says 1.0.0, README says 0.1.0 alpha.
flags: release notes and bundle filenames can drift from crate metadata

objects.title: manual QA surfaces
objects.recommendation: run before claiming end-to-end release readiness
objects.summary: Fn/Ctrl hotkeys, accessibility, microphone permission, Whisper download, paste, orb visibility, plugin loading, rewrite provider, and bundle launch need real runtime checks.
objects.evidence: Static tests cover contracts, not macOS permissions, hardware, or visible app behavior.
flags: do not call this validated without real runtime evidence

rules.title: active source rule
rules.body: Default to src-tauri and src-ui for live behavior. Treat docs and root src/ as supporting evidence only after checking the runtime path.

rules.title: privacy rule
rules.body: Local Whisper stays first. Cloud rewrite stays opt-in. Rewrite failure falls back to raw local transcript.

rules.title: release rule
rules.body: Do not claim release readiness from cargo gates alone. Zana needs visible tray/orb, permission, paste, and bundle launch evidence.

actions.title: rewrite privacy policy for opt-in cloud rewrite
actions.summary: PRIVACY.md must disclose local transcription, opt-in rewrite, provider endpoint/API key storage, optional screenshot context, and fallback behavior.

actions.title: resolve version drift before tagging
actions.summary: Decide whether the release is 0.1.0 alpha or 1.0.0, then align Cargo, Tauri config, README, and release notes.

actions.title: label or archive egui docs
actions.summary: ARCHITECTURE, MIGRATION, and API should not present egui/no-web architecture as the current runtime while src-tauri and src-ui are active.

actions.title: split install guide for users and developers
actions.summary: Separate release install from source build, and replace destructive uninstall examples with safer wording.

actions.title: extend QA checklist
actions.summary: Add cloud rewrite toggle, screenshot context, local-only negative path, update banner, and artifact launch smoke checks.

actions.title: tighten plugin docs to live support
actions.summary: Clarify manifest and hook behavior versus placeholder dynamic loader and aspirational marketplace publishing.

actions.title: run actual Zana runtime smoke
actions.summary: Launch the app, verify tray/orb visibility, accessibility/mic prompts, record/transcribe/paste, preferences save, and optional rewrite fallback.
